finding.summary: AI journal prose explicitly details actively 'executing exit plan' at ticks 87, 89, and 90, contradicting the structured intent action which remains 'PREPARE_CALL' with no current position. This continues the critical latency regression where intent state logic fails to update correctly immediately following non-canonical exits, reproducing issues from prior builds (and earlier in this session at tick 50).
evidence: Tick 90, 10:59 AM. Intent action is 'PREPARE_CALL'. State: not in position. Last trade exited at 10:18 AM (tick 50).
Journal entries from 10:55 AM (tick 87) and 10:56 AM (tick 89) report 'Executing exit plan' and 'Continuing to execute exit' respectively, after recognizing a burst past the 750 wall.
finding.title: Persistence of Invalid Exit-Execution Intent Following Vehicle Failure Exit
finding.suggested_action: Fix critical latency causing incorrect 'HOLD' and 'OPEN_POSITION' logic to persist after un-planned trade exits. This regression has been reported in multiple builds.

evidence: Tick 110, 11:19 AM. Intent action is 'PREPARE_CALL'. Position is null.
Journal entry at 11:16 AM (tick 109) reads: 'Ready to actively manage exit on expansion towards FEP zone.' and 'Position remains highly favorable.'
finding.summary: The system continues to reproduce the critical logic regression where open-position exit management persists after a trade is closed. At tick 110, the AI journal references managing an exit and describes the position as 'highly favorable,' yet the structured position state has been null since the vehicle failure at tick 50 (10:18 AM). This reproduces INTENT_EXIT_EXECUTION_LATENCY_AFTER_VEHICLE_FAILURE_V1_3 reported at tick 90.
finding.suggested_action: Fix the high-priority latency causing open-position 'HOLD' and 'EXIT_MANAGEMENT' logic to persist in the structured intent models long after an unplanned exit.
finding.title: Persistent Open-Position Logic Following Vehicle Failure
